cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which description about Yves Gomes is true based on the provided text. The options are A, B, C, or D. First, I need to go through each option and check against the information given in the passage. Starting with option A: "He was born in India before his parents immigrated to America illegally." Wait, the passage says that Yves Gomes is an illegal immigrant who grew up in the US. It mentions he was taken to India when he was 14 months old by his parents who were deported more than a year ago. So if he was taken to India when he was 14 months old, that means he was born in the US. Because he left the US at 14 months, so he was born in the US. Therefore, option A says he was born in India, which is incorrect. So A is false. Option B: "His parents were forced to leave India more than a year ago." Wait, the passage says that he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. Wait, no, the sentence says: "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. They were deported more than a year ago." So he left India when he was 14 months old, meaning his parents took him to the US. Then they were deported (back to India) more than a year ago. So the parents were deported from the US to India. Therefore, the statement in B is incorrect because it says "forced to leave India", but actually they left India when he was 14 months old, and were later deported from the US. So B is incorrect. The parents were deported from the US, not forced to leave India. Option C: "He wants to finish school in America and become a nurse in India after graduation." The passage says that Gomes wants to finish school and become a doctor, and he wants to be a US citizen. He mentions wanting to study here and go to medical school, and that if he has to return to India it would feel like it's all gone to waste. So he doesn't want to become a nurse in India, he wants to stay in America and become a doctor. Therefore, option C is false. Option D: "He is optimistic about the passage of the DREAMAct." The passage states that Yves Gomes hopes the DREAMAct won't be blocked and that he wants it to pass. The last paragraph says he hopes it won't happen (blockage) and he wants to finish school and become a citizen. So D is correct. The DREAMAct is the legislation that
